Party_Details.Name: Tuitiongenius Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Tuitiongenius Pte Ltd was incorporated by Eugene and Keng
Eugene signed an employment agreement with TGPL
TGPL commenced business operations at Clementi Centre
TGPL opened a second branch at Bedok
Eugene resigned as a director of TGPL

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company can claim damages for contraventions of employment agreements and management obligations by a former director
Party_Details.Rule: A company may seek damages if it can prove that a director contravened employment agreements and management obligations, causing financial harm
Party_Details.Application: The former director breached his employment agreement and fiduciary duties by secretly running a competing personal tuition business, retaining company revenues, and diverting a student for personal gain.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that Eugene breached cl 12 of the Employment Agreement in respect of Timothy but dismissed other claims due to oral agreements permitting Eugene's actions.

Party_Details.Name: Toh Yew Keat

Facts.Text: Eugene was a director and shareholder of Tuitiongenius Pte Ltd
Eugene signed an employment agreement with TGPL
Eugene registered Economics at Tuitiongenius as a sole proprietorship
Eugene incorporated Economics at Tuitiongenius Pte Ltd
Eugene resigned as a director of TGPL

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a former director is legally responsible for contravening employment agreements and management obligations by engaging in competing business activities
Party_Details.Rule: A director must not engage in activities that conflict with their obligations to the company unless explicitly permitted by agreement
Party_Details.Application: There was no breach of fiduciary or contractual duties because the founding shareholders had a clear, binding oral agreement that explicitly permitted the director to conduct his personal tuition business and retain its revenues.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that oral agreements existed allowing Eugene to conduct his personal tuition business, thus negating claims of contravention except for the breach involving Timothy.

Party_Details.Name: Toh Yew Keat

Facts.Text: Eugene registered Economics at Tuitiongenius as a sole proprietorship
Eugene incorporated Economics at Tuitiongenius Pte Ltd
Eugene received a letter of demand from the plaintiff's solicitors on 2016-02-05

Party_Details.Issue: Harassment and conspiracy through the initiation of a lawsuit
Party_Details.Rule: A claim for conspiracy requires proof of a combination to cause harm with the predominant purpose of causing injury
Party_Details.Application: The defendants by counterclaim engaged in a lawful means conspiracy by initiating a baseless and oppressive lawsuit with the predominant purpose of causing financial ruin to his competing business.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found insufficient evidence to prove that the predominant purpose of the lawsuit was to cause financial harm to Eugene.

Party_Details.Name: Tuitiongenius Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Tuitiongenius Pte Ltd was incorporated by Eugene and Keng
Eugene resigned as a director of TGPL

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company can be held legally responsible for initiating a lawsuit with the intent to cause harm to a former director
Party_Details.Rule: A company is legally responsible for conspiracy if it can be shown that the predominant purpose of a lawsuit was to cause harm rather than to resolve legitimate business disputes
Party_Details.Application: The lawsuit was a legitimate commercial action pursued in good faith to enforce the company's contractual rights and protect its interests, completely negating any allegation of a conspiracy to injure.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence that the predominant purpose of the lawsuit was to cause harm, dismissing the counterclaim.
